step_by_step_reasoning: The whiteboard shows a central orchestration engine running on EC2 ('Fugue Conductor') inside a customer AWS account. It interacts with S3 to download compiled infrastructure declarations/compliance policies, DynamoDB for state persistence, KMS for encryption, and STS for secure cross-account access. SQS is used to receive messages from the outside world. The EC2 instance scans and manages infrastructure in target external AWS accounts (Account 1 and Account 2, modeled as VPCs). Real-time drift alerts are sent to SNS.

graph.name: Automated Drift Detection and Baseline Enforcement with Fugue
graph.notes: Fugue Conductor runs as an EC2-based orchestration engine inside a dedicated customer account to provision, continuously scan, and remediate resource drift across multiple target AWS accounts via IAM cross-account roles.

nodes.name: Operations Agent
nodes.notes: Administrators and operators triggering actions or sending instructions to the management pipeline.

nodes.name: Fugue Conductor
nodes.notes: Orchestration engine running in an Auto Scaling Group that provisions infrastructure and scans for drift.

nodes.name: Policy & Declarations Storage
nodes.notes: Saves compiled infrastructure declarations and custom/standard compliance policies.

nodes.name: State Persistence
nodes.notes: Maintains persistent state data for the orchestration engine.

nodes.name: Inbound Message Queue
nodes.notes: Handles inbound instruction messages sent to the conductor.

nodes.name: Drift Notifications
nodes.notes: Publishes real-time alerts if a resource drift is detected.

nodes.name: KMS Encryption
nodes.notes: Secures orchestration data and SQS message payloads.

nodes.name: STS & IAM
nodes.notes: Authenticates and generates cross-account credentials to manage multiple target accounts.

nodes.name: AWS Account 1
nodes.notes: First target client account managed and monitored for drift by the central conductor.

nodes.name: AWS Account 2
nodes.notes: Second target client account managed and monitored for drift by the central conductor.

edges.notes: Delegates temporary security credentials to the conductor.
Conductor downloads the compiled policy definitions.
Conductor reads and writes execution state data.
Pulls configuration instructions from the queue.
Scans and enforces compliant states in Target Account 1.
Scans and enforces compliant states in Target Account 2.
